notes: Without skill output is notably shorter — useful but thin. With skill output is more complete and actionable.

The 'NexusPoint Positioning section with a specific best angle' assertion is the most discriminating — with_skill passes 3/3, without_skill fails 3/3. The skill reliably enforces structured positioning output; without it the model produces unstructured 'possible angles' bullets.
Research quality (web scraping) and factual accuracy assertions are NOT discriminating — both configurations pass them consistently. The skill does not improve research depth, only output structure.

Watch-outs / size mismatch flagging (eval 1 gymshark) is partially discriminating: without_skill does flag it but buries it at the end; with_skill surfaces it in a dedicated section with clearer framing.
Without-skill runs are faster (-7.6s avg) and use fewer tokens (-4,666 avg) because they produce thinner output. With-skill output is longer and more structured — this is the expected tradeoff.
Eval 2 (casual phrasing, minimal info) shows the skill is most valuable under ambiguity — with_skill maintains 100% while without_skill still passes 75% but produces a noticeably thinner brief.
